description_long: This object is a vintage console television with a large, boxy wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features a woodgrain finish, with a control section on the right side that includes a large rotary dial and several push buttons, likely for channel and volume adjustment. Below the screen, there is a fabric-covered speaker grille, typical of mid-20th-century television designs. The sides and back are finished in matching dark wood veneer, with a vent panel visible on one side for heat dissipation. The television stands on four tapered wooden legs, giving it a mid-century modern aesthetic. The overall construction suggests a heavy, solid build, with most of the volume occupied by the wooden cabinet and internal electronics.
description: A vintage wooden console television with a convex glass screen, side control panel, and fabric speaker grille, standing on tapered legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, woodgrain control panel with rotary dial and buttons, and fabric speaker grille below.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain finish and a rectangular vent panel near the back.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, uninterrupted woodgrain surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: Right side view with a continuous woodgrain finish and no visible vents or controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick woodgrain bezel, dark brown, rectangular, with a control panel on the right side.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of buttons and a rotary dial on the front right panel.
